Title: Reading Berlin 1900
description: The great cities at the turn of the century were mediated by words—newspapers, advertisements, signs, and schedules—by which the inhabitants lived, dreamed, and imagined their surroundings. In this study of the newspaper page as a key text of urban modernism, Peter Fritzsche analyzes how reading and writing shaped the experience of Imperial Berlin and anticipated a modernist sensibility characterized by discontinuity, instability, and transience.

Title: Billy Wilder on Assignment: Dispatches from Weimar Berlin and Interwar Vienna
description: Before he became one of Hollywood's most celebrated directors, Billy Wilder worked as a journalist in Berlin and Vienna. This volume collects his early newspaper writings from the late 1920s and early 1930s, offering vivid reports on nightlife, cinema, culture, and society during the Weimar era and interwar Austria.

Title: The Captive Press in the Third Reich
description: Oron J. Hale's study examines the transformation of the German press under National Socialism. It describes how the Nazi regime established control over newspapers through censorship, institutional restructuring, and political supervision, turning the press into an instrument of propaganda and state authority.

Title: Press and Politics in the Weimar Republic
Journalists between Hitler and Adenauer: From Inner Emigration to the Moral Reconstruction of West Germany
The Language of Nazi Genocide: Linguistic Violence and the Struggle of Germans of Jewish Ancestry
description: This book investigates how the Nazi regime used language to define, categorize, and exclude Jews in Germany. It analyzes official terminology, legal language, and public discourse to show how linguistic practices facilitated persecution and genocide. It also examines how Jews in Germany responded to and resisted these classifications.

Title: Arab Media: Power and Weakness
description: This book analyzes the development of Arab media systems and the relationship between media, political power, and social change. It examines government control, censorship, satellite television, and the challenges faced by journalists in Arab countries, highlighting both the limitations and growing influence of Arab media in the region.

Title: News Media in the Arab World: A Study of 10 Arab and Muslim Countries
description: This book provides a comparative study of news media in ten Arab and Muslim countries. It examines the structure, content, and political context of newspapers, television, and radio in the region, highlighting the ways governments and political elites influence media coverage. The book also addresses issues of media freedom, censorship, and the evolving role of media in shaping public opinion.

Title: The Jewish Press in the Third Reich
Winter Into Spring: The Czechoslovak Press and the Reform Movement, 1963-1968
Press, Radio and Film in West Germany, 1945-1953
Newspapers in Central and Eastern Europe / Zeitungen in Mittel- und Osteuropa: Papers presented at an IFLA conference held in Berlin, August 2003
description: This volume contains papers presented at an IFLA conference held in Berlin in August 2003. It brings together studies on newspapers in Central and Eastern Europe, including historical development, library collections, preservation, bibliography, and access to newspaper resources in the region.

Title: News in Times of Conflict: The Development of the German Newspaper, 1605-1650

The New Yorker (March 6, 2017)
description: The March 6, 2017 issue of The New Yorker contains reporting, essays, criticism, fiction, cartoons, and cultural commentary. The issue includes sections such as politics, international affairs, arts, books, and humor.

Title: The New Yorker (April 10, 2017)
description: The April 10, 2017 issue contains articles on politics, culture, literature, arts, and humor, featuring reporting, essays, and criticism from staff writers and contributors.

Title: The New Yorker (April 3, 2017)
description: The April 3, 2017 issue includes political reporting, cultural commentary, fiction, essays, and cartoons, following the magazine's standard structure for news, features, and literary content.

Title: The Mystery of the Empty Safe
description: This book is a children's mystery story in the Boxcar Children series. It follows the children as they investigate the case of an empty safe and uncover clues leading to a surprising resolution.

Title: The Birth of Vietnamese Political Journalism: Saigon, 1916-1930
description: Cornell University Press description:This book explores the emergence of modern journalism in colonial Saigon, examining how Vietnamese journalists navigated censorship, public opinion, and political activism under French rule.

Title: An Arsenal for Democracy: Media Accountability Systems (Hampton Press Communication Series. Communication Alternatives)
The Italian anti-Fascist press (1919-1945); from the legal opposition press to the underground newspapers of World War II
description: The book explores the history of the Italian press opposing Mussolini's regime, tracing the evolution from legal opposition newspapers to clandestine underground publications during World War II.
